title: Testing, Fault Injection, Release, and Production Defense · Mastery Check
summary: A production review compares near-miss decisions for testing, fault injection, release, and production defense and accepts only bounded behavior, causal changed-case evidence, and an explicit AWS transfer gate. Planned artifact: Record independent decisions and explanations for production file-delivery defense dossier. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Use table, property, and fuzz tests for key, range, policy, lifecycle, state-machine, checksum, retry, and cache invariants with changed cases.
Use fakes and emulators for deterministic feedback while listing unsupported AWS semantics and assigning controlled integration tests.
Gate formatting, vet, static analysis, tests, fuzz seeds, race, goroutine and body leaks, benchmarks, profiles, reproducible build, and dependency review.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: S3, CloudFront, and Go Diagnose the hidden failure · Quiz Compatible Release Defense
steps.instruction:
quiz case — A quiz Go file-delivery team handles case 4823 during a private media release. Build deterministic Go evidence to gate release on deterministic, Go runtime, controlled AWS, fault, load, restore, and rollback evidence; cap bytes, time, attempts, concurrency, and spend before starting work; then double concurrent transfers and keep process resource limits intact. Browser code uses pure values and state transitions only. AWS SDK packages, networks, S3, CloudFront, KMS, IAM, WAF, databases, credentials, host state, race and leak checks, load, faults, restore, billing, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates. Competency probe: Canary immutable artifacts and infrastructure, compare changed behavior and cost, preserve rollback data and policies, rehearse recovery, and defend residual risk.

The visible result looks correct, but its invariant or value contract is unverified. What must happen next?

Evidence target: S3, CloudFront, and Go Diagnose the hidden failure · Quiz Compatible Release Defense.
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for canary immutable artifacts and infrastructure, compare changed behavior and cost, preserve rollback data and policies, rehearse recovery, and defend residual risk. For S3, CloudFront, and Go Diagnose the hidden failure · Quiz Compatible Release Defense, keep Compatible Release Defense connected to executable Go storage and delivery decision evidence.
